title: [HW] Fix IndexBitWidthConstraint when the size is 1
body:
Currently `array_get ` doesn't work well when the length of array is 1. For example,
```mlir
hw.module @array1(%arr: !hw.array<1xi1>) -> (o1: !hw.array<1xi1>) {
    %false = hw.constant false
    %0 = hw.array_get %arr[%false] : !hw.array<1xi1>
    hw.output %arr : !hw.array<1xi1>
  }
```
will produce 
```
foo.mlir:3:28: error: use of value '%idx' expects different type than prior uses: 'i0' vs 'i1'
  %val = hw.array_get %arr[%idx] : !hw.array<1xi1>
                           ^
foo.mlir:2:3: note: prior use here
  %idx = hw.constant 0 : i1
```
This is because `IndexBitWidthConstraint` asserts that the width of index is equal to `llvm::Log2_64_Ceil(length)` which is equal to 0 when length is 1. This PR modifies to use `length == 1? 1 : llvm::Log2_64_Ceil(length)`.
